data.title: Uncovering Potential: UMB Financial's Earnings Preview
data.description:
UMB Financial (NASDAQ:UMBF) is gearing up to announce its quarterly earnings on Tuesday, 2026-01-27. Here's a quick overview of what investors should know before the release.

Analysts are estimating that UMB Financial will report an earnings per share (EPS) of $2.71.

UMB Financial bulls will hope to hear the company announce they've not only beaten that estimate, but also to provide positive guidance, or forecasted growth, for the next quarter.

New investors should note that it is sometimes not an earnings beat or miss that most affects the price of a stock, but the guidance (or forecast).

Overview of Past Earnings

During the last quarter, the company reported an EPS beat by $0.21, leading to a 3.21% drop in the share price on the subsequent day.

Here's a look at UMB Financial's past performance and the resulting price change:

UMB Financial Share Price Analysis

Shares of UMB Financial were trading at $123.16 as of January 23. Over the last 52-week period, shares are up 1.11%. Given that these returns are generally positive, long-term shareholders should be satisfied going into this earnings release.

data.title: Google Rolls Out Free SAT Practice Exams In Gemini: Stocks To Watch
data.description:
Alphabet Inc‘s (NASDAQ:GOOG) Google is quietly launching on demand practice exams for standardized tests in Gemini, starting with the SAT. Several stocks could be impacted. Here’s what you need to know.

  • Alphabet stock is gaining positive traction. What’s driving GOOG shares up?

Google Rolls Out On-Demand SAT Practice

Google said in a social media post that it is launching full-length, on-demand practice exams within the Gemini app, starting with the SAT. The tech giant is offering the practice tests at no cost.

The company said the practice exams are built using rigorously vetted content developed in partnership with The Princeton Review. Gemini provides immediate feedback after completion, highlighting areas where users performed well and where additional study may be needed.

To access the feature, users can prompt Gemini by saying, "I want to take a practice SAT test."

Education Stocks To Watch

Coursera, Inc. (NYSE:COUR)

Coursera offers online courses, professional certificates and degree programs from universities and companies across a wide range of subjects. Coursera shares are trading 1.23% lower at $6.42 at the time of publication.

Nerdy Inc. (NYSE:NRDY)

Nerdy operates an online learning platform that connects students with live tutors for K–12, test prep and professional skills. Nerdy shares are trading 1.79% lower at $1.10, according to data from Benzinga Pro.

Docebo (NASDAQ:DCBO)

Docebo provides cloud-based learning management software used by enterprises to deliver employee training and development programs. Docebo shares were down 0.22% at $18.56 at last check.

Duolingo, Inc. (NASDAQ:DUOL)

Duolingo offers a mobile-first platform for language learning and digital literacy through short, gamified lessons. Duolingo shares are trading 3.37% lower at $150.80 at the time of writing.

Pearson, Plc (NYSE:PSO)

Pearson develops educational content, assessments and digital learning tools for students, educators and institutions worldwide. Pearson shares are up slightly, last up 0.30% at $13.21.

Stride, Inc. (NYSE:LRN)

Stride provides online education programs, curriculum and support services for K–12 students and adult learners. Stride shares were down 0.33% at $73.02 at the time of publication.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: Piper Sandler Potential To Outperform Peer Growth Is Limited: Analyst
data.description:
Piper Sandler Companies (NYSE:PIPR) is poised to benefit from accelerating M&A activity, especially in the U.S. banking sector, but has limited potential for outsized earnings growth, analysts say.

The Piper Sandler Companies Analyst: BofA Securities analyst Ebrahim Poonawala initiated coverage with an Underperform rating and price target of $385.

The Piper Sandler Companies Thesis: The company's core franchise is skewed towards smaller-cap deals and has less exposure to tech, which could "limit potential for outsized EPS growth vs. peers," Poonawala said in the initiation note.

Check out other analyst stock ratings.

Piper Sandler Companies ranks second on deals announced with a value of less than $1 billion and last among boutique investment banks on average deal size of $1.6 billion, versus the peer average of $3.9 billion, he added.

While the strategy of focusing on smaller deals is good for incremental growth, it limits the company's ability to generate growth higher than peers, the analyst stated.

The stock currently trades at multiples in line with peers, despite Piper Sandler's below-peer-average earnings growth and a higher PEG (price/earnings-to-growth) ratio, he added.

PIPR Price Action: Shares of Piper Sandler Companies had declined by 1.52% to $354.89 at the time of publication on Monday.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Here's How Much You Would Have Made Owning IBM Stock In The Last 5 Years
data.description:
IBM (NYSE:IBM) has outperformed the market over the past 5 years by 7.73% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 21.08%. Currently, IBM has a market capitalization of $276.79 billion.

Buying $100 In IBM: If an investor had bought $100 of IBM stock 5 years ago, it would be worth $258.18 today based on a price of $296.12 for IBM at the time of writing.

IBM's Performance Over Last 5 Years

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.
